title: Direct evidence of sex not needed to prove adultery: Madras High Court
description: The Madras High Court recently held that direct evidence of sexual intercourse is not essential to establish adultery in matrimonial disputes.A Division Bench consisting of Justice CV Karthikeyan and Justice K Rajasekar held that circumstantial evidence such as public proximity between parties and a complaint by the paramour’s spouse can be sufficient to grant divorce.The High Court underscored the inherent difficulty in proving adultery through direct evidence.
